title: Bitcoin Edges Higher to $64.5K as Crypto Steadies; Logistics Costs Rising
excerpt: Bitcoin gains 1.24% to $64,527 as crypto steadies. FedEx rate increases pressure corporate supply chains in 2026.
content:
# MoneyTailors Daily Market Brief **Sunday, 14 June 2026** --- ## HOOK HEADLINE Bitcoin Edges Higher to $64.5K as Crypto Steadies; Logistics Costs Rising ---

Cryptocurrency markets show modest gains this Sunday, with Bitcoin climbing 1.24% to $64,527 and Ethereum holding near $1,676. The broader digital asset space reflects cautious positioning ahead of the week. In traditional markets, logistics operators report rising parcel shipping costs as major carriers implement 2026 rate increases, signaling inflationary pressure in supply chain operations.

## Cryptocurrency: Modest Sunday Gains Across Major Coins Bitcoin trades at $64,527, up 1.24% over the past 24 hours, while Ethereum edges forward 0.14% to $1,675.70. Solana outperforms with a 1.51% gain to $68.40, and BNB rises 1.21% to $611.32. The moves reflect steady, if unremarkable, weekend momentum as major cryptocurrencies consolidate recent levels without directional conviction. ## Macro: FedEx Rate Increases Put Pressure on Corporate Cost Structures Freight and logistics costs are rising in 2026 as FedEx implements rate increases affecting parcel shipping nationwide. Broussard Logistics reports that companies are now conducting detailed audits of parcel expenses to manage the impact of these carrier rate adjustments. Broussard Logistics notes that rising transportation costs are a material concern for supply chain managers seeking to protect margins amid inflationary pressure in logistics. --- **What to Watch:** • Monitor corporate earnings guidance for supply chain cost commentary as companies report Q2 results this week • Track broader inflation data releases to assess whether transportation cost increases will feed into consumer pricing • Watch for additional carrier announcements regarding 2026 rate adjustments that could compound logistics expense pressures ---
